message:
feat(wa-core): unify notification interface with centralized redaction

Introduce notifications.rs as the single source of truth for notification
payloads across all delivery backends (desktop, webhook, future channels).

Architecture changes:
- New NotificationPayload struct consolidates event_type, pane_id,
  timestamp, summary, description, severity, agent_type, confidence,
  quick_fix, and suppression counts into one canonical format
- NotificationSender trait defines the contract for all notification
  backends with async send() returning NotificationDelivery results
- NotificationDelivery tracks success/failure, rate limiting status,
  error messages, and delivery records per sender
- Redactor integration at payload construction ensures secrets are
  removed before reaching any notification channel (critical for
  webhook payloads going over the network)

Backend implementations:
- DesktopNotifier now implements NotificationSender, building payloads
  via from_detection() before dispatching to platform-specific commands
- Added urgency_from_str helper for severity string to Urgency enum
- WebhookEndpointConfig gains matches_event_type() for event filtering
  without requiring full Detection structs (useful for replay/test)

Benefits:
- Consistent payload format across desktop, webhook, and future backends
- Security: redaction happens once, at payload creation, not per-backend
- Testability: from_detection_with_redactor() allows custom redactors
- Extensibility: adding new backends only requires implementing the trait

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-15ds","title":"[EPIC] Local RPC socket for robot/MCP (auth + caps)","description":"## Background
Robot/MCP APIs are powerful; local tools need a secure and ergonomic way to connect without parsing CLI output.

## Goals
- Provide a local IPC server over Unix domain socket / named pipe
- Authenticate with short-lived tokens and capability scopes
- Keep parity with robot/MCP schemas

## Non-Goals
- Remote network exposure (handled by distributed mode)

## Considerations
- Must be disabled by default
- Requires strict redaction and audit logging

## Success Criteria

- Token and scope checks enforced
- Unit + e2e tests for auth and schema parity","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1t2b","title":"[EPIC] Saved searches + scheduled alerts","description":"## Background
Operators repeatedly run the same search/event filters to track specific agent states. Today this is manual and easy to forget.

## Goals
- Persist named searches (query + filters + scope) for reuse
- Allow scheduled runs that emit notifications when results appear
- Integrate with CLI/TUI/web dashboards without duplicating search logic

## Non-Goals
- New query language (reuse existing FTS syntax)
- External alert delivery (handled in notification channels epic)

## Considerations
- Saved search must be safe to execute (no side effects)
- Scheduling should be rate-limited and observable
- Outputs must be redacted per safety config

## Success Criteria
- Users can create/list/run/delete saved searches
- Scheduled alerts fire with clear context and can be disabled
- Full unit + e2e test coverage with verbose logs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:03.835297223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:03.835297223Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2ipp","title":"Scheduler: weighted capture + budget enforcement","description":"## What
Implement weighted scheduling and budget enforcement in the capture loop.

## Why
Keeps critical panes responsive while preventing runaway CPU/storage use.

## How
- Extend scheduler to compute next capture by priority weight
- Enforce per-pane and global budgets with clear reasons
- Emit throttle events for visibility

 {"id":"bd-2v4t","title":"Add alt-screen detection via escape sequence parsing in pattern engine","description":"## Overview

Implement alt-screen (alternate screen buffer) detection by parsing terminal escape sequences in the captured output stream, eliminating the need for Lua's \`pane:is_alt_screen_active()\`.

## Background

Terminal applications (vim, less, htop, etc.) switch to the alternate screen buffer using standardized escape sequences:

\`\`\`
Enter alt-screen: ESC [ ? 1049 h   (smcup - start mode cup)
Leave alt-screen: ESC [ ? 1049 l   (rmcup - reset mode cup)
\`\`\`

These sequences are emitted by the application and captured in wa's output stream. By detecting them, we know the alt-screen state without asking WezTerm via Lua.

## Technical Details

### Escape Sequence Variants
Some terminals/apps use alternative sequences:
\`\`\`
Primary (most common):
  ESC [ ? 1049 h    — enter
  ESC [ ? 1049 l    — leave

Alternative (xterm compatibility):
  ESC [ ? 47 h      — enter (older)
  ESC [ ? 47 l      — leave (older)

Combined with cursor save/restore:
  ESC [ ? 1049 h ESC [ ? 1 h   — enter + application cursor keys
\`\`\`

### Implementation Location

Add to **crates/wa-core/src/patterns.rs** or create new **crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs**:

\`\`\`rust
/// Tracks alt-screen state by parsing escape sequences
pub struct ScreenStateTracker {
    /// Current alt-screen state per pane
    alt_screen_active: HashMap<u64, bool>,
}

impl ScreenStateTracker {
    /// Process captured output, update alt-screen state
    pub fn process_output(&mut self, pane_id: u64, output: &[u8]) {
        // Scan for ESC [ ? 1049 h/l sequences
        // Update self.alt_screen_active[pane_id]
    }
    
    /// Query current alt-screen state
    pub fn is_alt_screen(&self, pane_id: u64) -> bool {
        self.alt_screen_active.get(&pane_id).copied().unwrap_or(false)
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Points

1. **Ingest loop** (ingest.rs): After capturing pane output, pass to ScreenStateTracker
2. **Pattern detection** (patterns.rs): Can use alt-screen state in rule matching
3. **IPC server** (ipc.rs): StatusUpdate can report alt-screen from tracker

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ScreenStateTracker struct implemented
- [ ] Detects ESC[?1049h as alt-screen enter
- [ ] Detects ESC[?1049l as alt-screen leave
- [ ] Handles sequences split across capture boundaries
- [ ] Unit tests with various escape sequence formats
- [ ] Integration with ingest loop
- [ ] Benchmark showing minimal overhead

## Test Cases

1. **Basic enter/leave**: Single ESC[?1049h followed by ESC[?1049l
2. **Partial sequence**: Sequence split across two output chunks
3. **Nested/repeated**: Multiple enters without leave (state stays active)
4. **Mixed content**: Normal text interspersed with sequences
5. **Alternative sequences**: Test ESC[?47h/l variants
6. **No sequences**: Normal output doesn't change state

## Performance Considerations

- Use memchr or Aho-Corasick for efficient ESC scanning
- Avoid regex for this (too slow for every output chunk)
- Track state change timestamps for debugging

## Files to Modify

- crates/wa-core/src/lib.rs — add mod screen_state
- NEW: crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs — ScreenStateTracker
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs — integrate tracker
- crates/wa-core/src/ipc.rs — StatusUpdate uses tracker

## References

- XTerm control sequences: https://invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html
- ECMA-48 (ANSI escape codes)
- Existing pattern engine: crates/wa-core/src/patterns.rs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenForest","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:43:56.669479578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:16:02.822658602Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:16:02.822570318Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2v4t","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:43:56.686539882Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":74,"issue_id":"bd-2v4t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented ScreenStateTracker in crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs:

 {"id":"bd-2xe4","title":"[EPIC] Phase 2: Vendored WezTerm Native wa Integration (Optional Future)","description":"## Overview

This sub-epic is for **future consideration** if Phase 1 (removing STATUS_UPDATE_LUA) is insufficient or if we need even tighter integration with WezTerm. It involves modifying WezTerm's Rust source directly to add native wa event delivery.

**Priority P2**: Only pursue if Phase 1 doesn't fully solve the performance issue or if real-time event delivery (vs polling) becomes critical.

## Context

The user mentioned they can "directly modify WezTerm in Rust" and want to be "thoughtful about how we do that so we can easily merge our changes as WezTerm gets updated by its author nightly."

This epic captures the design and implementation approach for vendored WezTerm modifications.

## Why Vendored WezTerm?

### Benefits
1. **Zero Lua overhead** — events delivered in native Rust
2. **Sub-millisecond latency** — no process spawning, direct IPC
3. **Richer data** — access to WezTerm internals not exposed to Lua
4. **Custom optimizations** — e.g., only track wa-monitored panes

### Costs
1. **Maintenance burden** — must rebase on upstream WezTerm frequently
2. **Complexity** — two codebases to understand
3. **Upstream divergence risk** — changes might conflict with WezTerm updates
4. **Build complexity** — users must use vendored WezTerm build

## Design Principles for Upstream Compatibility

### 1. Feature-Gate Everything
\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
mod wa_events;
\`\`\`

All wa-specific code is behind a feature flag. Default WezTerm builds unchanged.

### 2. Trait-Based Extension Points
\`\`\`rust
pub trait WaEventSink: Send + Sync {
    fn on_pane_output(&self, pane_id: u64, data: &[u8]);
    fn on_pane_state_change(&self, pane_id: u64, state: PaneState);
    fn on_user_var_changed(&self, pane_id: u64, name: &str, value: &str);
}
\`\`\`

Define traits that can be implemented externally. WezTerm calls these if registered.

### 3. Minimal Touchpoints
Modify as few WezTerm files as possible:
- config/src/lib.rs — add wa_event_sink config option
- mux/src/pane.rs — add event emission at key points
- wezterm/src/main.rs — initialize wa integration if configured

### 4. No Breaking Changes
Never modify existing WezTerm behavior. wa integration is purely additive.

### 5. Document All Changes
Each modified file gets a comment block explaining the wa addition.

## High-Level Architecture

\`\`\`
WezTerm Process                           wa Process
┌────────────────────────────┐            ┌─────────────────┐
│  Terminal Emulation        │            │  wa watch       │
│         │                  │            │       │         │
│         ▼                  │            │       ▼         │
│  #[cfg(feature="wa")]      │            │  IPC Server     │
│  WaEventEmitter            │═══════════▶│  (Unix Socket)  │
│         │                  │  events    │       │         │
│         ▼                  │            │       ▼         │
│  Unix Socket Client        │            │  Event Handler  │
│  (non-blocking)            │            │                 │
└────────────────────────────┘            └─────────────────┘
\`\`\`

## Events to Emit

| Event | When | Data |
|-------|------|------|
| pane_output | New terminal output | pane_id, bytes |
| pane_state_change | Title/dimensions/alt-screen change | pane_id, new state |
| user_var_changed | OSC 1337 user-var set | pane_id, name, value |
| pane_created | New pane spawned | pane_id, domain, cwd |
| pane_destroyed | Pane closed | pane_id |

## Rebasing Strategy

WezTerm is updated nightly. To maintain our fork:

1. **Automated rebase CI** — nightly job that rebases our feature branch
2. **Conflict detection** — alert if rebase fails
3. **Minimal diff** — keep changes small to reduce conflicts
4. **Test suite** — run wa integration tests after each rebase

## Implementation Phases

1. **Design**: Define WaEventSink trait and IPC protocol
2. **Spike**: Create proof-of-concept in WezTerm fork
3. **Validate**: Measure performance improvement
4. **Productionize**: CI, tests, documentation
5. **Upstream proposal**: Consider proposing generic event hook to WezTerm upstream

## Success Criteria (if pursued)

- Feature-gated code compiles with default WezTerm
- Rebases cleanly on upstream for at least 30 days
- Measurable latency improvement vs polling
- No performance regression in non-wa WezTerm usage

## Decision Gate

Before starting this epic:
1. Complete Phase 1 (remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA)
2. Measure performance — is it good enough?
3. Identify use cases that REQUIRE real-time events
4. Estimate rebasing maintenance cost
5. User decision: proceed or not?

## References

- WezTerm source: https://github.com/wez/wezterm
- WezTerm event system: wezterm/mux/src/
- Feature flags: Cargo.toml [features]","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:02.671428898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:12.422725707Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xe4","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:12.422697574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xe4","depends_on_id":"bd-2oby","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:02.686305023Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-30gj","title":"Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA from setup.rs (with deprecation path)","description":"## Overview

Remove the STATUS_UPDATE_LUA constant and its injection into wezterm.lua from crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs. This is the core change that eliminates the Lua performance bottleneck.

## Current State

In setup.rs (lines 54-105), STATUS_UPDATE_LUA is defined:
\`\`\`rust
const STATUS_UPDATE_LUA: &str = r#"-- Forward pane status updates to wa daemon (rate-limited)
local wa_last_status_update = {}
local WA_STATUS_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS = 2000

wezterm.on('update-status', function(window, pane)
  // ... 50+ lines of Lua code
end)"#;
\`\`\`

This is injected via:
- \`create_wa_block()\` (line 837-838) — combines USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA + STATUS_UPDATE_LUA
- \`generate_ssh_domains_lua()\` (lines 708-711) — appends both snippets

## Changes Required

### 1. Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA Constant
Delete the entire constant definition (lines 49-105).

### 2. Update create_wa_block()
Before:
\`\`\`rust
fn create_wa_block() -> String {
    format!("{WA_BEGIN_MARKER}\\n{USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA}\\n\\n{STATUS_UPDATE_LUA}\\n{WA_END_MARKER}")
}
\`\`\`

After:
\`\`\`rust
fn create_wa_block() -> String {
    format!("{WA_BEGIN_MARKER}\\n{USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA}\\n{WA_END_MARKER}")
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Update generate_ssh_domains_lua()
Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA from the generated block:
\`\`\`rust
// Line 708-711, remove:
output.push_str(STATUS_UPDATE_LUA);
output.push('\\n');
\`\`\`

### 4. Update Test Assertions
Several tests check for STATUS_UPDATE_LUA content:
- test_create_wa_block_format() — update assertions
- test_generate_ssh_domains_block_includes_hosts_and_snippets() — remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA assertion

### 5. Migration Path for Existing Users

Users who already ran \`wa setup\` have STATUS_UPDATE_LUA in their wezterm.lua. Options:

**Option A: Silent removal on next setup**
- \`wa setup\` replaces the entire WA-managed block
- Old STATUS_UPDATE_LUA code gets removed automatically
- Simplest, but users don't know what changed

**Option B: Explicit migration command (Recommended)**
- Add \`wa setup --upgrade\` or \`wa setup migrate\`

Implement the wa-side Unix socket listener that receives events from vendored WezTerm's native integration. This replaces the Lua→CLI→IPC path with direct socket communication.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-3gon (Protocol design)

## Implementation

### 1. Event Listener Module

Create \`crates/wa-core/src/native_events.rs\`:

\`\`\`rust
use std::path::PathBuf;
use tokio::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream};
use tokio::sync::broadcast;

/// Events received from native WezTerm integration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum NativeEvent {
    PaneOutput {
        pane_id: u64,
        data: Vec<u8>,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    StateChange {
        pane_id: u64,
        title: String,
        rows: u16,
        cols: u16,
        is_alt_screen: bool,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    UserVarChanged {
        pane_id: u64,
        name: String,
        value: String,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    PaneCreated {
        pane_id: u64,
        domain: String,
        cwd: Option<String>,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    PaneDestroyed {
        pane_id: u64,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
}

/// Listener for native WezTerm events.
pub struct NativeEventListener {
    socket_path: PathBuf,
    listener: UnixListener,
    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<NativeEvent>,
}

impl NativeEventListener {
    /// Create and bind the event listener socket.
    pub async fn bind(socket_path: PathBuf) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Remove stale socket if exists
        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&socket_path);
        
        // Create parent directory
        if let Some(parent) = socket_path.parent() {
            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
        }
        
        let listener = UnixListener::bind(&socket_path)?;
        let (event_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1024);
        
        Ok(Self {
            socket_path,
            listener,
            event_tx,
        })
    }
    
    /// Subscribe to events.
    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<NativeEvent> {
        self.event_tx.subscribe()
    }
    
    /// Run the event listener loop.
    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        loop {
            let (stream, _) = self.listener.accept().await?;
            let event_tx = self.event_tx.clone();
            
            tokio::spawn(async move {
                if let Err(e) = handle_connection(stream, event_tx).await {
                    tracing::warn!("Native event connection error: {}", e);
                }
            });
        }
    }
}

async fn handle_connection(
    stream: UnixStream,
    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<NativeEvent>,
) -> Result<(), Error> {
    use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, BufReader};
    
    let reader = BufReader::new(stream);
    let mut lines = reader.lines();
    
    while let Some(line) = lines.next_line().await? {
        match parse_event(&line) {
            Ok(event) => {
                let _ = event_tx.send(event);  // Ignore if no subscribers
            }
            Err(e) => {
                tracing::debug!("Failed to parse native event: {}", e);
            }
        }
    }
    
    Ok(())
}

fn parse_event(json: &str) -> Result<NativeEvent, Error> {
    let value: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(json)?;
    
    match value.get("type").and_then(|t| t.as_str()) {
        Some("pane_output") => Ok(NativeEvent::PaneOutput {
            pane_id: value["pane_id"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
            data: base64::decode(value["data"].as_str().unwrap_or(""))?,
            timestamp: value["ts"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
        }),
        Some("state_change") => Ok(NativeEvent::StateChange {
            pane_id: value["pane_id"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
            title: value["state"]["title"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
            rows: value["state"]["rows"].as_u64().unwrap_or(24) as u16,
            cols: value["state"]["cols"].as_u64().unwrap_or(80) as u16,
            is_alt_screen: value["state"]["is_alt_screen"].as_bool().unwrap_or(false),
            timestamp: value["ts"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
        }),
        // ... other event types
        _ => Err(Error::UnknownEventType),
    }
}

\`\`\`rust
impl Watcher {
    pub async fn run_with_native_events(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        let native_listener = NativeEventListener::bind(
            self.config.native_event_socket.clone()
        ).await?;
        
        let mut native_rx = native_listener.subscribe();
        
        // Spawn listener task
        let listener_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
            native_listener.run().await
        });
        
        loop {
            tokio::select! {
                // Existing poll loop
                _ = self.poll_interval.tick() => {
                    self.poll_panes().await?;
                }
                
                // Native events
                Ok(event) = native_rx.recv() => {
                    self.handle_native_event(event).await?;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    async fn handle_native_event(&mut self, event: NativeEvent) -> Result<()> {
        match event {
            NativeEvent::PaneOutput { pane_id, data, .. } => {
                // Feed to pattern detection
                self.pattern_engine.process(&data);
                // Feed to screen state tracker
                self.screen_tracker.process_output(pane_id, &data);
            }
            NativeEvent::StateChange { pane_id, is_alt_screen, .. } => {
                // Update pane state directly (no polling needed)
                self.pane_states.update_alt_screen(pane_id, is_alt_screen);
            }
            NativeEvent::UserVarChanged { pane_id, name, value, .. } => {
                // Same handling as IPC user-var events
                self.handle_user_var(pane_id, &name, &value).await?;
            }
            // ... other events
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.706395892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.705890700Z","close_reason":"Added wa_meta version compatibility checks + schema gating","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-storage","area-tests","area-ux","phase-1","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-03j","title":"Workflow: handle_claude_code_limits (Claude Code session/token limits)","description":"# Workflow: handle_claude_code_limits

## Purpose
Handle Claude Code specific limit scenarios:
- Session length limits (context window exhaustion)
- Token budget limits (per-session or per-day)
- Rate limiting

## Detection Patterns
```
# Context exhaustion
"I'll need to summarize our conversation"
"context window is getting full"

# Session limits
"session has reached its limit"
"Please start a new conversation"

# Rate limiting
"You've sent too many messages"
"Please wait before sending"
```

## Workflow Steps

### Step 1: Detect and classify limit type
```rust
match detection.extracted["limit_type"] {
    "context" => handle_context_limit(),
    "session" => handle_session_limit(),
    "rate" => handle_rate_limit(),
}
```

### Step 2: Context limit handling
1. Capture current task state from transcript
2. Wait for natural breakpoint (prompt boundary)
3. Suggest `/compact` or manual conversation restart
4. If auto-handling enabled: send `/compact` command
5. After compaction, re-inject critical context (AGENTS.md)

### Step 3: Session limit handling
1. Save session state (transcript, current file, task progress)
2. Notify user of limit reached
3. Provide command to start fresh session with context restore

### Step 4: Rate limit handling
1. Calculate wait time from message
2. Pause workflow execution
3. Resume when rate limit window passes
4. No aggressive retry

## Safety Constraints
- Never auto-restart without user config allowing it
- Preserve user's work (save transcript)
- Respect Claude Code's /compact semantics

## Configuration
```toml
[workflows.handle_claude_code_limits]
auto_compact = false  # Require explicit enable
save_transcript = true
notify_on_limit = true
```

## Testing
- Fixture: context limit detection patterns
- Fixture: session limit detection patterns
- Fixture: rate limit detection patterns
- Unit: workflow step logic
- E2E: limit detection → notification flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Claude Code limit types detected
- [ ] Context limits handled via /compact when enabled
- [ ] Session limits preserve state
- [ ] Rate limits respected (no retry spam)
- [ ] User notified of limits","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:37.390261502Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:41:12.121839506Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:41:12.121769976Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-tests","area-ux","area-workflows","phase-3","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-03j","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":154,"issue_id":"wa-03j","author":"BrightBrook","text":"BrightBrook: Implemented HandleClaudeCodeLimits workflow in workflows.rs. 3 steps: check_guards, check_cooldown (10min), classify_and_record.
